comment.body: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump's new tariff policy will be delayed, with a potential start date of April 1. CNBC's senior Washington correspondent, Eamon, revealed on the social media platform 'X' that he was informed the policy announced by President Trump today will not take effect immediately. Instead, there will be a delay of several months, with April 1 as a possible implementation date, though this remains tentative. Today's announcement will be in the form of a presidential memorandum rather than an executive order, and the U.S. Trade Representative's office will be responsible for developing the next steps.
